method: Independent spot-check: for each sampled app, the verifier (a separate Claude session, not the original research subagent) re-fetched primary sources directly via WebFetch/WebSearch and compared against the subagent's original claim, without looking at the subagent's reasoning first.

checks.detail: composio.dev/toolkits/salesforce shows 179 tools / 7 triggers, OAuth2/S2S OAuth2 — exact match.
api.docs.dealcloud.com confirms API key generation requires admin-granted 'API' capability; no free/trial path documented.

usepylon.com/pricing meta description references 'free trial today', which the original pass missed and stated flatly as no trial. All paid tiers still require 3+ seat minimums, so the buildability verdict (ready_with_workaround) is unaffected, but the note was corrected.

checks.claim_checked: blocked, requires seller account + app approval (days-weeks)
checks.detail: Amazon's own docs confirm developer approval can take 'a few minutes to a few weeks'; verdict and blocker reasoning hold, timeline wording was slightly imprecise (had said 'days to weeks').

checks.claim_checked: CLI-only, no hosted API, no auth
checks.detail: GitHub README confirms pip/pipx/Docker local install, no API/account of any kind.

checks.claim_checked: paid_plan_required — API access needs Business/Enterprise plan
checks.detail: Smartsheet Help Center and third-party guides confirm API tokens cannot be generated on free/trial; Business or Enterprise plan required.

checks.claim_checked: trial_self_serve — Sandbox instant, Production requires review
checks.detail: plaid.com/docs/api confirms Sandbox client_id/secret issued on signup; Production access is requested separately via Dashboard (review step).

checks.claim_checked: Official MCP server exists at mcp.stripe.com
checks.detail: docs.stripe.com/mcp confirms the server, OAuth or restricted-key auth, 40+ API methods exposed.

checks.claim_checked: paid_plan_required — Commerce API needs Commerce Advanced plan
checks.detail: developers.squarespace.com confirms Commerce Advanced plan requirement for custom apps; a minor nuance (Extensions available on any plan) wasn't captured in the original note.

checks.claim_checked: paid_plan_required — API restricted to Enterprise workspaces
checks.detail: help.otter.ai and independent pricing writeups confirm the Public API and MCP server are Enterprise-only, gated via account manager.
composio.dev/toolkits/github actually shows 846 tools / 46 triggers, not ~893. Numbers corrected in the dataset; toolkit-exists verdict unaffected.

checks.claim_checked: Official hosted MCP server, described generically as 'in public beta'
checks.detail: mcp.twilio.com is explicitly read-only (docs/spec search, 'does not execute API calls'); the API-executing MCP is a separate twilio-labs/mcp project. Original note conflated the two — corrected to distinguish them.

checks.claim_checked: trial_self_serve — Test Account instant, production needs dev-token review
checks.detail: developers.google.com confirms Test Account access can start immediately (sometimes pending approval) while Basic/Standard access requires a reviewed application.

checks.claim_checked: Ads API requires Standard-access demo-video review
checks.detail: Source URL returned 404 on re-fetch; could not independently re-confirm this specific claim within the verification budget. Flagged as unverified rather than assumed correct.
docs.composio.dev/toolkits/reducto explicitly states 'Composio Managed App Available? N/A' — no working toolkit exists yet. This was a genuine factual error in the first pass, now corrected to false.

summary.note: Strict accuracy counts any drift (even a corrected tool count or a conflated MCP claim) as a miss on pass 1. Directional accuracy counts only claims where the buildability verdict itself would have been wrong. One claim (Pinterest) could not be re-verified within budget and is disclosed as unverified rather than silently trusted.
